title: Real-World Generative Product Examples
concept: From AI art tools to product photo generation to game asset creation, generative media now powers a wide range of real commercial products. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: The Generative Media Capstone.'

exercises.prompt: Where can we find generative media tools being used today?

options.text: In real commercial products like games and ads

exercises.explain: Generative media tools are now used to create real assets for marketing, video games, and product design.

cards.title: AI in the Wild
cards.body: Have you ever wondered how your favorite new indie video games or online shops create so many cool visuals so fast? They aren't using magic—they are using generative AI products to create stunning art and game assets in seconds!

cards.title: Commercial Generative Media
cards.body: Generative product tools are real-world applications that use AI models to create commercial images, game objects, or advertising designs. Instead of starting from scratch, creators use these tools to speed up their work and brainstorm new looks.

cards.title: The Toy Factory Analogy
cards.body: Imagine a toy shop where, instead of buying pre-made plastic blocks, you tell a magic box, "Give me a blue castle wall with moss on it," and it instantly hands it to you. That's how game studios use generative tools: instead of hand-sculpting thousands of tiny rocks or trees, they describe them, and the AI builds them.

cards.title: Instant Studio Backgrounds
cards.body: An online clothing brand needs to show a backpack in a cozy cabin, on a sunny beach, and in a busy city. Instead of paying for three expensive photoshoots, they upload one photo of the backpack to a generative design app and swap the backgrounds instantly with text prompts!

cards.title: Will Humans Disappear?
cards.body: Some people think AI tools will completely replace human artists and designers. In reality, these tools act like super-powered assistants. Humans are still needed to guide the AI, choose the best results, and add the final creative touch.

cards.title: Studio vs. AI Generation
cards.body: See how businesses switch from traditional production methods to AI-assisted workflows to save time and resources.

right.text: Uploading a product photo and using AI to generate infinite virtual studio backdrops in seconds.

left.text: Renting a physical studio, hiring models, and spending days shooting a single product setup.

exercises.prompt: Why do video game creators use generative AI tools?

options.text: To replace the game code entirely so it runs without computers.
To quickly draft concept art and generate textures for game worlds.
To prevent players from playing the game offline.
To physically manufacture the game consoles.

exercises.explain: Game developers use generative tools to quickly create prototype designs and 3D textures, allowing them to build worlds much faster.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Real-world design agencies use generative AI products to help brainstorm and create advertisements.
exercises.explain: Generative tools are widely used by professional graphic designers and developers to speed up their creative process.

exercises.prompt: Complete the sentence about how online shops use generative AI.
exercises.sentence: Online shops use generative AI to instantly swap product backgrounds to save money on photoshoots.
exercises.explain: E-commerce tools let sellers change the background of a product photo instantly to make it look like it was taken in a professional studio.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following images was NOT created using a generative AI product?

options.text: Image A: An ultra-detailed digital landscape generated from a text prompt.
Image B: A product photo with an AI-generated beach background.
Image C: A hand-drawn sketch made with physical ink on paper.

exercises.explain: Image C is created by a human artist using traditional paper and ink, while Images A and B are classic examples of generative AI outputs.

exercises.prompt: How are real companies successfully using generative media products today?

options.text: Generative AI is only useful for hobbyists and cannot be used in real business products.
Every single pixel in modern video games must still be drawn by hand without templates.
Companies use AI to generate product backgrounds and game assets to save time and spark ideas.
AI art tools have completely replaced human designers and no longer require human input.

exercises.explain: Using generative AI for media is no longer just a fun experiment; it's a core tool in modern games, marketing, and design. Next up: The Generative Media Capstone.
